title: Transactional Outbox, Inbox, and Sagas · Spiral Systems Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs transactional outbox, inbox, and sagas from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Dual Write Failure Matrix · changed case · Review Dual Write Failure Matrix
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Go messaging team handles case 9288 during race-enabled integration test with duplicate delivery. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Which action demonstrates Dual Write Failure Matrix without dropping earlier contracts?

steps.why: Retrieving Saga Compensation through the concrete a Go outbox relay and saga recovery model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Saga Compensation, keep Saga Compensation connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Saga Compensation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Saga Compensation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Saga Compensation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go messaging team handles case 8053 during consumer shutdown drill with one blocked handler goroutine. Build deterministic Go evidence to compose domain mutation, outbox record, confirmation, and ambiguous-effect recovery; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Saga Compensation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A compensation always restores the world to its exact state before the original action. In Go, a goroutine that publishes after commit closes the dual-write failure window.
